lessons.title: Lesson 1 Packing for a Business Trip

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What should I bring on this business trip?
sentences.zh: 这次出差我应该带什么？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Haven't you been on a business trip before?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, this is my first time.
sentences.zh: 没有，这是我第一次出差。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You should bring your laptop, cell phone, and chargers.
sentences.zh: 你应该带上笔记本电脑、手机和充电器。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Any other advice?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Bring a carry-on bag only, so you don't have to wait for your luggage.
sentences.zh: 只带一个随身行李箱，这样就不用等托运行李了。

lessons.title: Lesson 2 What to Pack on a Business Trip
lessons.titleEn: What to Pack on a Business Trip

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm nervous about this trip.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm meeting with the CFO.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Wow. That would make me nervous too.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What should I pack?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Well, you should definitely bring your laptop and tablet.
sentences.zh: 嗯，你一定要带上笔记本电脑和平板电脑。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know. What else?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Bring a nice suit.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I don't own a suit.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Well, you better go shopping then.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Wow, Colleen! You look great!
sentences.zh: 哇，科琳！你看起来棒极了！

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Thanks! I'm going on a business trip today.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Where are you going?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: San Francisco. Our corporate headquarters.
sentences.zh: 旧金山，我们的公司总部。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Meeting any important people?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yeah, all of the top management.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Well, you're definitely dressed to impress.
sentences.zh: 嗯，你这身打扮绝对能让人印象深刻。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What are you reading?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: A book about traveling etiquette in foreign countries.
sentences.zh: 一本关于国外旅行礼仪的书。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm going on a business trip next week.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Where are you going?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: To one of our offices in Saudi Arabia.
sentences.zh: 去我们在沙特阿拉伯的一个办事处。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That should be interesting.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Have you been there?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, I'm scheduled to go next month.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm reading about proper etiquette while I'm there.
sentences.zh: 我在了解在那边应该注意的礼仪。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Give me some tips after you return from your trip.
sentences.zh: 你出差回来后给我分享一些经验吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm a little nervous about this business trip.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm meeting with the vice president of the company.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I heard he is a drinker.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: So what?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't drink at all.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm afraid he's going to think I'm boring.
sentences.zh: 我怕他会觉得我很无聊。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Then tell jokes.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I heard he likes funny people.
sentences.zh: 我听说他喜欢幽默的人。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you ready for the business trip?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yeah, I'm a little nervous.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Is this your first business trip?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I just don't know any of these people.
sentences.zh: 不是，我只是不认识这些人。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you have any advice for me?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Just socialize. Get to know them. Go out for drinks one night.
sentences.zh: 多交际，多了解他们，找个晚上一起喝一杯。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Actually, two of our clients are taking me out for drinks the first night.
sentences.zh: 实际上，第一天晚上就有两个客户要请我喝酒。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's good. Don't stress about it so much.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I want to make a good first impression.
sentences.zh: 我想留下好的第一印象。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Then don't get drunk.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What if I get nervous and drink too much?
sentences.zh: 如果我一紧张喝多了怎么办？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Then you'll have more things to worry about.
sentences.zh: 那你就有更多烦心事了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What are you going to do on your business trip?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm not sure.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What's the schedule?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't know.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Our clients haven't sent me anything yet.
sentences.zh: 客户还没有给我发任何东西。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You're having a good attitude about it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm trying to be flexible.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When is your business trip?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Tomorrow.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Who are you meeting with?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: One of the managers, Kevin Johnson.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Have you met him?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, he's a nice guy, but he's very disorganized.
sentences.zh: 见过，他人很好，但做事很没条理。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's good to know. Any advice?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Be flexible because he changes his mind often.
sentences.zh: 要灵活一点，因为他经常改变主意。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That can be annoying.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, it can, but have a good attitude about it.
sentences.zh: 确实，但你要保持好的心态。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Because he's the CEO's son.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to make an emergency trip to our headquarters tonight.
sentences.zh: 我今晚需要紧急去总部一趟。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: There are no direct flights tonight. There are only non-direct flights.
sentences.zh: 今晚没有直飞航班，只有中转航班。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How many layovers?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: There is one layover in San Francisco for an hour.
sentences.zh: 在旧金山中转一次，停留一小时。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's fine. Can you book that flight, please?
sentences.zh: 可以。请帮我订那个航班好吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Do you need a ride to the airport?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No. I will park my car at the airport.
sentences.zh: 不用，我自己开车去机场停车。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to change my flight departure.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: When do you need to go?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to leave tomorrow morning.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Can it be a non-direct flight?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I would prefer a direct flight.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: There is one direct flight leaving tomorrow morning at 7 a.m.
sentences.zh: 明天早上7点有一班直飞航班。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's $100 more expensive.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'll take it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When will you be returning?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Don't change the return flight.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Okay.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hey Todd, this is Rob. My flight has been delayed. I won't be there on time.
sentences.zh: 嘿，托德，我是罗布。我的航班延误了，我没法按时到了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I guess I'll have to do the presentation alone.
sentences.zh: 看来我得一个人做演示了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you prepared to do that?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I have no choice.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can we postpone the meeting?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, the clients are catching a flight right after the meeting.
sentences.zh: 不行，客户会后就要赶飞机。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Lucy, where is Todd?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: He's in the conference room.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: He's been waiting for you.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Could you tell him my flight has been delayed?
sentences.zh: 你能告诉他我的航班延误了吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: He's not going to be happy.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: He's been waiting for an hour.
sentences.zh: 他已经等了一个小时了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: My flight isn't leaving for another hour.
sentences.zh: 我的航班还有一个小时才起飞。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay, I'll let him know.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Tell him I need to reschedule the meeting.
sentences.zh: 告诉他我需要重新安排会议。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't think I can tell him now.
sentences.zh: 我觉得现在没法告诉他。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: He's not there.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think he left.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'll call him on a cell phone.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Your business trips have earned you enough frequent flyer miles for a free airline ticket.
sentences.zh: 你出差累积的飞行里程已经够兑换一张免费机票了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Really? That's great!

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Our company lets their employees use the free airline ticket for personal use.
sentences.zh: 我们公司允许员工将免费机票用于个人用途。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Wow! I didn't know that.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You should probably use it during your next vacation.
sentences.zh: 你可以在下次休假时用掉它。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I think I will ask my wife where she wants to go.
sentences.zh: 我想问问我妻子想去哪里。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Good idea.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hi, Vicky. This is Isabelle. I'm the company's travel coordinator.
sentences.zh: 嗨，薇琪。我是伊莎贝尔，公司的差旅协调员。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Hi, Isabelle. Is there a problem?
sentences.zh: 嗨，伊莎贝尔。有什么事吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No. I see that you take quite a few business trips a month.
sentences.zh: 没什么问题。我看到你每个月出差挺频繁的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, I do.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I noticed that you're not signed up for frequent flyer miles.
sentences.zh: 我注意到你还没有注册飞行里程计划。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I haven't signed up yet.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Please sign up before your next trip. You can earn miles and get a free trip anywhere in the country.
sentences.zh: 请在下次出差前注册。你可以累积里程，兑换国内任意目的地的免费行程。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I didn't know that.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's easy to sign up. You can do it online.
sentences.zh: 注册很简单，在网上就可以办理。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will have to do that today since I have another business trip at the end of the week.
sentences.zh: 我今天就得注册，因为这周末还有一次出差。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This is the front desk. How can I help you?
sentences.zh: 这里是前台。请问有什么可以帮您？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: This is Janet Lee from room 112.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hello, Ms. Lee. How can I help you?
sentences.zh: 您好，李女士。有什么可以帮您的？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I paid for a room with an attached office, but this hotel room has no office.
sentences.zh: 我订的是带办公室的房间，但这间房没有办公室。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm sorry about that. I will change your room right away.
sentences.zh: 非常抱歉，我马上给您换房。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Great. I'm on a business trip. So an office is important to me.
sentences.zh: 太好了。我在出差，办公室对我来说很重要。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm not happy with my room.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: May I ask what is wrong?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I asked for a room with a view. This room has no view.
sentences.zh: 我要的是有景观的房间，但这间房什么景观都没有。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry, but we do not have any more rooms available tonight.
sentences.zh: 抱歉，今晚已经没有空房了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When I made the reservation, I paid for a room with a view.
sentences.zh: 我预订时付的是景观房的价格。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I understand. I'm very sorry, but all of the rooms are occupied.
sentences.zh: 我理解。非常抱歉，但所有房间都住满了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This is unacceptable.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We will discount your room rate.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Our company does a lot of business with this hotel.
sentences.zh: 我们公司和这家酒店有很多业务往来。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will be telling my boss about this.
sentences.zh: 我会跟我老板反映这件事。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: May we offer you a free stay next time?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It will be a room with a view. I will guarantee it.
sentences.zh: 一定给您安排景观房，我保证。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Is this all the luggage from this flight?
sentences.zh: 这就是这次航班所有的行李了吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I can't find my luggage.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It looks like your luggage is lost.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need my suitcase.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I have a business meeting today and my suit is in it.
sentences.zh: 我今天有商务会议，西装在里面。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm very sorry.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: If we locate it, we will send it to you right away.
sentences.zh: 如果我们找到行李，会立刻送到您那里。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to report lost luggage.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm very sorry that happened.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What do I need to do?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Please fill out this lost luggage form.
sentences.zh: 请填写这份行李丢失登记表。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have very important business papers in my suitcase.
sentences.zh: 我的行李箱里有非常重要的商务文件。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I understand. I am very sorry.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Will you contact me as soon as you find it?
sentences.zh: 找到后能第一时间联系我吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes. We will send it to you right away.
sentences.zh: 会的，我们会立刻寄给您。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm staying in a hotel downtown.
sentences.zh: 我住在市中心的一家酒店。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We will contact you as soon as we locate your suitcase.
sentences.zh: 我们一找到您的行李箱就联系您。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Is anyone picking you up at the airport?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I'm going to rent a car.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The boss approved your rental car?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: He didn't approve a rental car during my business trip.
sentences.zh: 我出差时他可没批准我租车。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I found a great deal for a rental car.
sentences.zh: 我找到了一个很划算的租车价格。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you on the phone?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yeah, but I'm on hold.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you have time for lunch?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Not right now. I'm trying to get a free upgrade on my rental car.
sentences.zh: 现在不行，我在试着免费升级租来的车。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you having any luck?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: The rental car agent is talking to her supervisor right now.
sentences.zh: 租车客服正在跟她的主管沟通。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How are you negotiating?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I told the agent that our company does a lot of business with this rental company.
sentences.zh: 我跟客服说我们公司和这家租车公司有很多业务往来。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You're right. We do.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Kyle, hold on. The agent is back on the phone.
sentences.zh: 凯尔，等一下，客服回来了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think the company is in trouble.
sentences.zh: 我觉得公司遇到麻烦了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What kind of trouble?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Financial trouble.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What makes you think that?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I overheard Lou talking about bankruptcy laws.
sentences.zh: 我无意中听到卢在谈论破产法。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We can't lose our jobs. I just bought a home.
sentences.zh: 我们不能丢工作，我刚买了房。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think the company has finally made a profit. That's great news.
sentences.zh: 我觉得公司终于盈利了。这是好消息。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We're all getting raises and bonuses this year.
sentences.zh: 今年我们都会加薪和发奖金。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That makes me very happy. Me too. We haven't had raises in four years.
sentences.zh: 那让我很高兴。我也是。我们已经四年没加薪了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We've never had bonuses.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's right. If the company is doing well financially, we should definitely receive some benefits.
sentences.zh: 没错，如果公司财务状况良好，我们肯定应该得到一些福利。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I agree. We work really hard. Let's celebrate over drinks after work.
sentences.zh: 我同意，我们工作真的很辛苦。下班后一起去喝一杯庆祝一下吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Have you met the company's new CEO?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, have you?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, she's very nice.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Really? I heard she was very mean.
sentences.zh: 真的吗？我听说她很刻薄。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She asked us a lot of questions about employee happiness and concerns.
sentences.zh: 她问了我们很多关于员工幸福感和担忧的问题。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I heard if she doesn't like what you say, she will fire you.
sentences.zh: 我听说如果她不喜欢你说的话，就会解雇你。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Gloria is stressed out.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The new CTO is visiting our office.
sentences.zh: 新任首席技术官要来我们办公室。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: So, what's the big deal?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's a big deal to Gloria.
sentences.zh: 这对格洛丽亚来说是一件大事。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: She's one of the managers.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Gloria is a great boss.
sentences.zh: 格洛丽亚是个很棒的老板。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: She wants to make a good first impression.
sentences.zh: 她想给人留下良好的第一印象。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She hasn't met the new CTO yet?
sentences.zh: 她还没见过新任首席技术官？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No. This will be her first time meeting him.
sentences.zh: 不，这将是她第一次见到他。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No wonder Gloria is nervous.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm planning our company's hospitality event.
sentences.zh: 我正在策划我们公司的招待活动。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What are you doing this year?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We've invited a couple of professional golfers.
sentences.zh: 我们邀请了几位职业高尔夫球手。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We're going to do a celebrity golf tournament.
sentences.zh: 我们将举办一场名人高尔夫锦标赛。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Wow, that's going to be a lot of fun.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I just need to hear back from the golfers.
sentences.zh: 我只是想听听高尔夫球手的回复。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What if no one accepts the invitation?
sentences.zh: 如果没有人接受邀请，该怎么办？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Then I am in big trouble.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you going to the hospitality event?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes! I have a few clients attending.
sentences.zh: 是的！我有几个客户参加。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Great! This year we're doing a raffle and gift bags.
sentences.zh: 太棒了！今年我们要做抽奖活动和礼品袋。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Raffle and gift bags? Isn't that a little childish?
sentences.zh: 抽奖和礼品袋？这不是有点幼稚吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The raffle prize is a sports car and the gift bags are filled with jewelry.
sentences.zh: 抽奖奖品是一辆跑车，礼品袋里装满了珠宝。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you serious? Can our company afford this?
sentences.zh: 你是认真的吗，我们公司能负担得起吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The CEO wants to keep our clients happy.
sentences.zh: 首席执行官希望让我们的客户满意。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's definitely going to keep them happy.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It'll probably attract more clients to our company, too.
sentences.zh: 这也可能会吸引更多的客户加入我们的公司。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do we have a company dress code?
sentences.zh: 我们有公司着装要求吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes. On Mondays through Thursdays, we have to wear business attire.
sentences.zh: 是的。周一至周四，我们必须穿着商务服装。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What about Fridays?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: On Fridays, we can dress casually.
sentences.zh: 在周五，我们可以穿着随意。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What does casual dress mean?
sentences.zh: 休闲连衣裙是什么意思？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You can wear jeans and T-shirts, but not shorts.
sentences.zh: 你可以穿牛仔裤和T恤，但不能穿短裤。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do we have a company dress code?
sentences.zh: 我们有公司着装要求吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, the president of the company doesn't care how you dress.
sentences.zh: 不，公司总裁不在乎你的穿着。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Really? You can wear jeans and t-shirts or shorts?
sentences.zh: 真的吗？你可以穿牛仔裤、T恤或短裤吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yeah, he only cares about the quality of your work.
sentences.zh: 是的，他只关心你的工作质量。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's great. We had to wear suits to work every day at my old job.
sentences.zh: 太好了。我以前的工作每天都要穿西装去上班。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Even on Fridays?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yeah, our president was very conservative.
sentences.zh: 是的，我们的总裁非常保守。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Was that uncomfortable?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, wearing pantyhose every day is very uncomfortable.
sentences.zh: 是的，每天穿连裤袜很不舒服。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I really like my work schedule.
sentences.zh: 我真的很喜欢我的工作时间表。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Do you work flexible hours?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, I can choose my own hours.
sentences.zh: 是的，我可以选择自己的工作时间。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I wish I could work flexible hours.
sentences.zh: 我希望我可以灵活的工作时间。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why don't you?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I can't. I'm in accounting, so I have to work regular business hours.
sentences.zh: 我不能。我在会计部门，所以我必须正常工作时间。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Were you here yesterday?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I don't come into the office every day.
sentences.zh: 不，我不是每天都来办公室。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you work from home?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, I just make sure I work 40 hours a week.
sentences.zh: 是的，我只是确保每周工作40小时。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You're so lucky.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know. My boss gave me a flexible schedule because I am a single parent.
sentences.zh: 我知道。我的老板给了我一个灵活的时间表，因为我是单亲父母。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You have a very understanding boss.
sentences.zh: 你有一个非常善解人意的老板。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know. I'm very lucky.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I would love to work from home. The traffic to work is terrible.
sentences.zh: 我很乐意在家工作。上班的交通很糟糕。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Maybe you should ask your boss about working a flexible schedule too.
sentences.zh: 也许你也应该问问你的老板是否可以灵活安排工作时间。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Veronica, when is your baby due?
sentences.zh: 维罗妮卡，你的宝宝预产期是什么时候？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Next month. I'm going on maternity leave.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How long will you be away?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: About three months.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We'll miss you at the office.
sentences.zh: 办公室里大家都会想你的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Thanks. I'll keep in touch by email.
sentences.zh: 谢谢。我会用邮件和大家保持联系。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Will you come back part-time after that?
sentences.zh: 那之后你会回来做兼职吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, two days a week at first.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did Catherine have her baby?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, she's on maternity leave right now.
sentences.zh: 是的，她现在正在休产假。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How long will she be gone?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Three months.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's all? That's so short.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know, she's sad about it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Is it paid maternity leave?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's good.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Catherine wants to take an extra month of maternity leave.
sentences.zh: 凯瑟琳想多休一个月产假。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you have time for lunch?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I have a lunch meeting with the boss today.
sentences.zh: 不，我今天和老板有个午餐会议。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The boss? Is something wrong?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, we're going to discuss my raise.
sentences.zh: 不，我们要讨论我的加薪。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Good luck.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Thanks, I'll need it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm so busy.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you done with the report?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, I've been in meetings all day.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's why I haven't seen you.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我没有见过你。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yeah, I have another meeting at 4 p.m.
sentences.zh: 是的，下午4点我还有另一个会议。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why do you have so many meetings?
sentences.zh: 你为什么有这么多会议？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The managers are discussing annual employee reviews and raises.
sentences.zh: 经理们正在讨论年度员工审查和加薪。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Is it that time of the year?
sentences.zh: 是一年中的那个时候吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yeah, the end of the year is always the busiest.
sentences.zh: 是的，年底总是最繁忙的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You're right, it's almost 4 p.m. You'd better go.
sentences.zh: 你说得对，快下午4点了，你最好走了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm meeting with an architect today.
sentences.zh: 我今天要见一位建筑师。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why? Are you remodeling your house?
sentences.zh: 为什么？你在装修房子吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No. We are going to remodel the office.
sentences.zh: 不，我们将对办公室进行改造。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's a good idea. The layout is not very good.
sentences.zh: 这是个好主意。布局不是很好。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know. Some areas are too small and some are just a waste of space.
sentences.zh: 我知道。有些区域太小，有些只是浪费空间。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I agree. Can I get a bigger office?
sentences.zh: 我同意。我可以得到一个更大的办公室吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This office layout is confusing.
sentences.zh: 这种办公室布局令人困惑。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I agree.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Our clients have a hard time finding the exit and our offices.
sentences.zh: 我们的客户很难找到出口和我们的办公室。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know. Bill got lost the other day looking for the restroom.
sentences.zh: 我知道，比尔前几天在找洗手间的时候迷路了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Should we redesign it?
sentences.zh: 我们应该重新设计它吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That would be a good idea.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I don't think we have the budget for it.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们没有足够的预算。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's too bad.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: But we should do something soon.
sentences.zh: 但我们应该尽快采取行动。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know. The layout looks like a maze.
sentences.zh: 我知道，布局看起来像个迷宫。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What is the vacation policy?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You earn one week of paid vacation after you've worked at the company for one year.
sentences.zh: 在公司工作满一年后，你可以获得一周带薪休假。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I've only been here for nine months, but I'd like to take one week off next month.
sentences.zh: 我来了才九个月，但我想下个月休一周假。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Is it a medical situation?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No. My parents live in Italy, and it's their 50th wedding anniversary.
sentences.zh: 不是。我父母住在意大利，那天是他们结婚50周年纪念日。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You can take one week off with your supervisor's approval, but it won't be paid.
sentences.zh: 经主管批准你可以休一周，但不会是带薪的。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think we should change the vacation policy.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们应该改变休假政策。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Other companies have paid vacation. We do not.
sentences.zh: 其他公司也有带薪假期。我们没有。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We are a small company. We can't afford paid vacations.
sentences.zh: 我们是一家小公司。我们负担不起带薪假期。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Paid vacations would make employees work better.
sentences.zh: 带薪休假将使员工工作得更好。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: How?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Give employees good benefits and they will work harder.
sentences.zh: 为员工提供良好的福利，他们将更加努力工作。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You have a point, but we cannot afford it.
sentences.zh: 你有道理，但我们买不起。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This company is never going to grow if we don't take care of our employees.
sentences.zh: 如果我们不照顾员工，这家公司就永远不会成长。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I agree, but I don't make the rules.
sentences.zh: 我同意，但我不制定规则。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How do you balance your work life and your personal life?
sentences.zh: 你如何平衡你的工作生活和个人生活？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't bring my work home.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What do you mean?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Don't do more than you can handle.
sentences.zh: 不要做超出自己能力范围的事情。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What if you have a lot of work to do?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Then I stay at the office and finish it.
sentences.zh: 然后我留在办公室完成它。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When I go home, I focus on home only.
sentences.zh: 当我回家时，我只关注家里。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I wish I was like you.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm so stressed right now.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why? Is there something wrong?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm so busy at work.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm forgetting about my personal life.
sentences.zh: 我忘记了我的私人生活。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I understand. That happened to me, too.
sentences.zh: 我明白，这也发生在我身上。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: How do you balance it all out?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I just set a strict schedule.
sentences.zh: 我只是设定了一个严格的时间表。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Work is work, and home is home.
sentences.zh: 工作就是工作，家就是家。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's not that easy.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: But if you don't do that, you're going to get sick.
sentences.zh: 但如果你不这样做，你会生病的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I think that's my problem.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I feel sick all the time.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's arrange a meeting for next week.
sentences.zh: 我们安排下周开个会吧。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Sounds good. Tuesday at 11 a.m. works for me. Shall we meet in my office?
sentences.zh: 听起来不错。周二上午11点我有空。我们在我办公室见面好吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How about a lunch meeting instead?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Even better. Let's meet at the restaurant across the street. We can negotiate our business contract then.
sentences.zh: 更好。我们在街对面的餐厅见面吧。到时候我们可以谈一下商务合同。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Perfect. I'll book a table for two.
sentences.zh: 太好了。我来订一张两人的桌。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Great. Please send me the agenda before Tuesday.
sentences.zh: 好的。请在周二前把议程发给我。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Dan, we're excited to start working with you.
sentences.zh: 我们很高兴开始与你合作！

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Should we set up a meeting to discuss the details of the work?
sentences.zh: 我们应该安排一个会议来讨论工作的细节吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, that's a good idea.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: When are you free?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How about next Tuesday?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I have an all-day meeting on Tuesday. What about Wednesday?
sentences.zh: 我周二有一个全天的会议。周三呢？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm on a business trip on Wednesday. I will be back Friday morning.
sentences.zh: 我周三出差。我会在周五早上回来。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Friday works for me. Should we do a lunch meeting?
sentences.zh: 星期五适合我。我们应该开个午餐会吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Sure. I'll put it in my calendar.
sentences.zh: 当然，我会把它放在我的日历上。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Have a good business trip and I'll see you next Friday.
sentences.zh: 祝你出差愉快，下周五见。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you have some time this afternoon?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can you pick our client up from the airport?
sentences.zh: 你可以从机场接我们的客户吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Sure, what time?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to check the email for the flight schedule.
sentences.zh: 我需要查看航班时间表的电子邮件。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay, just let me know his airline and arrival time.
sentences.zh: 好的，请告诉我他的航空公司和抵达时间。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Could you pick our client up from the airport?
sentences.zh: 你能去机场接我们的客户吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Sure. What time is his flight?
sentences.zh: 当然，他的航班是什么时候？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: 5 p.m.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: 5 p.m.? That's rush hour.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know. I'm sorry about that.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Where do I drop him off?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: His hotel is downtown.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Downtown? I need to come back downtown during rush hour?
sentences.zh: 市中心？我需要在高峰时段回到市中心吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, please. Would you also be able to take him out to dinner?
sentences.zh: 是的，请。你也可以带他出去吃饭吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Dinner too?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes. You can borrow the company car.
sentences.zh: 是的。你可以借用公司的汽车。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hi, my name is Diane Taylor. I'm the office manager.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我叫戴安·泰勒。我是办公室经理。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Hi Diane. I'm Cody Smith. Nice to meet you.
sentences.zh: 嗨，戴安。我是科迪·史密斯。很高兴认识你。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Nice to meet you too. Please let me know if you have any questions.
sentences.zh: 我也很高兴认识你。如有任何疑问，请随时联系我。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will. I don't have any questions right now.
sentences.zh: 我会的。我现在没有任何问题。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Here is my business card. You can contact me by email, office phone, or cell phone.
sentences.zh: 这是我的名片。你可以通过电子邮件、办公室电话或手机联系我。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Great. I'm sure I will have lots of questions soon.
sentences.zh: 太好了。我相信我很快会有很多问题。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What kind of work do you do?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm an architect.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's interesting. I'm in construction.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I studied construction management in college.
sentences.zh: 我在大学学的是建筑管理。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We're looking for an architect for our firm.
sentences.zh: 我们公司正在找一名建筑师。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I've been interested in working on the construction side.
sentences.zh: 我一直对建筑方面的工作感兴趣。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can I have your business card? Here is mine.
sentences.zh: 能给我你的名片吗？这是我的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Can I send you my resume?
sentences.zh: 我可以把简历发给你吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, that would be great.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: My email address is on the card.
sentences.zh: 我的邮箱地址在名片上。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Thank you. I look forward to meeting with you again.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hi, my name is Bill. You must be Karen, our newest client.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我叫比尔。你一定是凯伦，我们最新的客户。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, I'm Karen. Nice to meet you, Bill.
sentences.zh: 是的，我是凯伦。很高兴认识你，比尔。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Nice to meet you too. I'm the head of marketing.
sentences.zh: 我也很高兴认识你。我是营销主管。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I think we will be working closely together.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, that's what I hear. Let me know if you have any questions.
sentences.zh: 是的，我也听说了。有问题随时告诉我。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will. My first question is, where is the restroom?
sentences.zh: 好的。我的第一个问题是，洗手间在哪里？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hi Vincent. I'm Natalie, your new client.
sentences.zh: 嗨，文森特。我是娜塔莉，你的新客户。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Nice to meet you, Natalie.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Nice to meet you too, Vincent.
sentences.zh: 我也很高兴认识你，文森特。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Have you taken a tour of the office yet?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, not yet.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Would you like me to show you around?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That would be great.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Let's start with my office. It's at the end of the hallway.
sentences.zh: 先从我的办公室开始吧。在走廊尽头。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You have a view of the mountains.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, it's a relaxing view in a stressful industry.
sentences.zh: 是的，在这个压力大的行业里，这样的风景让人放松。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I understand the stress part.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Kathy should be here soon. Is this your first trip to Seattle?
sentences.zh: 凯西应该很快就到。这是你第一次来西雅图吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It's a lovely city. I just don't like the rain.
sentences.zh: 这是个漂亮的城市。我只是不太喜欢下雨。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Where are you from?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm from Detroit. I work in the auto industry.
sentences.zh: 我来自底特律。我在汽车行业工作。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Really? I visited a factory there last year.
sentences.zh: 真的吗？我去年参观了那里的一家工厂。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Small world. Which company?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: A parts supplier near the river.
sentences.zh: 河边一家零部件供应商。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I might know them. Let's exchange cards later.
sentences.zh: 我可能认识他们。待会儿我们交换一下名片吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We're 15 minutes early for the meeting. Should we wait for everyone to arrive?
sentences.zh: 我们比会议时间早到了15分钟。要等大家都到吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay. So how do you like this weather? I don't like it at all.
sentences.zh: 好吧。你觉得这天气怎么样？我是一点都不喜欢。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Me too. It's been raining all week. Have you been out of town?
sentences.zh: 我也是。整周都在下雨。你出城去了吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, I just got back from Hawaii last night. I do miss the weather there.
sentences.zh: 是的，我昨晚刚从夏威夷回来。我确实想念那边的天气。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hawaii sounds amazing. Was it for business or vacation?
sentences.zh: 夏威夷听起来太棒了。你是出差还是度假？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: A bit of both. I went to a trade show and stayed an extra day.
sentences.zh: 两者都有。我参加了一个展会，多待了一天。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Lucky you. I haven't traveled for fun in years.
sentences.zh: 你真幸运。我好多年没出去玩过了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You should. A short trip really helps.
sentences.zh: 你该去。短途旅行真的很有帮助。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm quitting my job.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why? Are you unhappy here?
sentences.zh: 为什么？你在这里不开心吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No. I received a new business opportunity.
sentences.zh: 不是。我得到了一个新的商业机会。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Wow! That's great! Can you tell me about it?
sentences.zh: 哇！太好了！能跟我说说吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'll tell you all about it over lunch.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay. I'm free at noon.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We've all enjoyed working with you. It's been a lot of fun.
sentences.zh: 我们都很喜欢和你共事。这段时间很开心。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you offering me a job? We'd love to have you join our company.
sentences.zh: 你是在给我提供工作吗？我们很希望你能加入我们公司。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Wow, this would be a great business opportunity for me.
sentences.zh: 哇，这对我来说是个很好的职业机会。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: My secretary will email you the job description and our salary offer.
sentences.zh: 我的秘书会把职位描述和薪资方案发邮件给你。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When do you need an answer? We would like an answer by the end of the week.
sentences.zh: 你们什么时候需要答复？我们希望本周结束前能得到答复。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay, I will think about it carefully and let you know by Friday.
sentences.zh: 好的，我会认真考虑，周五前给你答复。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm sorry, but can we reschedule our meeting?
sentences.zh: 抱歉，我们的会议能改期吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Sure. Is something wrong?
sentences.zh: 当然可以。出什么事了吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: My husband is having surgery that day.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry to hear that.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's not serious, but I need to stay home with him.
sentences.zh: 不是大手术，但我需要在家陪他。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I understand. Let's reschedule when your husband is fully recovered.
sentences.zh: 我理解。等你丈夫完全康复了再重新安排吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can we reschedule the presentation?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Again? Why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We aren't ready.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We already rescheduled once. This will be the second time.
sentences.zh: 我们已经改过一次了。这将是第二次。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know, but we're just not ready.
sentences.zh: 我知道，但我们确实没准备好。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You've had two months to prepare for this.
sentences.zh: 你们有两个月的时间来准备。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I understand that, but we need more time.
sentences.zh: 我明白，但我们需要更多时间。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: The clients have been very patient.
sentences.zh: 客户们已经很有耐心了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Please, ask them for one more week.
sentences.zh: 拜托，再向他们要一周时间。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You have one week.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Has Carol signed the contract?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Not yet. We're still working on it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why don't we take her out to lunch and discuss things?
sentences.zh: 我们不如请她吃午饭谈谈？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Good idea. Maybe she has questions about the contract.
sentences.zh: 好主意。也许她对合同有疑问。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you know her favorite food or restaurant?
sentences.zh: 你知道她最喜欢的食物或餐厅吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know she loves French food.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Find the best French restaurant in the city.
sentences.zh: 找一家城里最好的法国餐厅。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What are you doing after work?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I have to take our clients out to dinner.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Where are you going to take them?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't know. Do you have any suggestions?
sentences.zh: 不知道。你有什么建议吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: There's a really good sushi place downtown.
sentences.zh: 市中心有一家很好的寿司店。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: The new one on Main Street?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yeah, that's the one.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I can't take them there.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Our clients own it.
sentences.zh: 那家店是我们客户开的。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you going to the cocktail party tonight?
sentences.zh: 今晚的鸡尾酒会你去吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes. I invited one of our clients.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Who?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Andrew.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Our new client? The good-looking one?
sentences.zh: 我们的新客户？那个很帅的？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Is he good-looking?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Don't act like you didn't notice.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did you go to the cocktail party last night?
sentences.zh: 昨晚的鸡尾酒会你去了吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I didn't see you there.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I was sick.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's too bad.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Something crazy happened there.
sentences.zh: 那里发生了件疯狂的事。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I heard about our new client.
sentences.zh: 我听说了我们新客户的事。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yeah, he got really drunk.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did our boss see him?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, she was not happy to see that.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: He must be really embarrassed this morning.
sentences.zh: 他今天早上一定很尴尬。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I had lunch with Sue today.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Your ex-girlfriend?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, we bumped into each other at the store.
sentences.zh: 是的，我们在商店碰到了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: She's back in town?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yeah, she moved back last month.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you going to see her again?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm single now, so I asked her on a date.
sentences.zh: 我现在单身，所以约她出去了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What did she say?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, we have more catching up to do.
sentences.zh: 她答应了，我们还有很多要叙旧的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Ask Sue if she keeps in touch with Karen, my ex-girlfriend.
sentences.zh: 问问苏是否还和凯伦有联系，凯伦是我的前女友。

lessons.title: Lesson 60 Catching Up with an Old Friend
lessons.titleEn: Catching Up with an Old Friend

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I was on the phone for an hour.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Who were you talking to for an hour?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Sam, an old friend from college.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: When is the last time you talked to him?
sentences.zh: 你上次跟他说话是什么时候？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: At college graduation.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Wow! That was 30 years ago.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We had a lot of catching up to do.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did you hear about Molly and Dan?
sentences.zh: 你听说莫莉和丹的事了吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, what's going on?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: They're getting a divorce.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Really? They've been married for 30 years.
sentences.zh: 真的吗？他们已经结婚30年了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Dan cheated on Molly.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That is awful!

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did you hear the office rumor?
sentences.zh: 你听到办公室的谣言了吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I didn't. What's happening?
sentences.zh: 不，我没有。发生了什么事？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: They're going to lay off several people this month.
sentences.zh: 他们这个月要解雇几个人。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Where did you hear this?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I heard it from Bill.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you worried?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes. I've only been here for six months.
sentences.zh: 是的，我才来这里工作了六个月。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Then I should be more worried than you.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why? Everyone likes you here.
sentences.zh: 为什么？这里的人都喜欢你。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I've only been here for four months.
sentences.zh: 我才来这里工作了四个月。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you remember when we were kids?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, you were mean to me.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, I wasn't.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, you were. You used to take my toys.
sentences.zh: 是的，你以前总是拿我的玩具。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I did? I'm sorry.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's okay. You're a great brother now.
sentences.zh: 没关系，你现在是个好哥哥了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you remember our 10th birthday?
sentences.zh: 还记得我们的10岁生日吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, we got a puppy that year.
sentences.zh: 是的，那年我们养了一只小狗。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I miss that puppy.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Me too. He had a great name.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Actually, I don't remember his name.
sentences.zh: 其实，我不记得他的名字了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: How could you forget his name? His name was Buster.
sentences.zh: 你怎么能忘记他的名字？他的名字叫巴斯特。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Oh, yeah. It was a sad day when Buster died.
sentences.zh: 哦，是啊，巴斯特去世那天很难过。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I never wanted another dog after that.
sentences.zh: 在那之后，我再也不想要另一只狗了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How old was Buster when he died?
sentences.zh: 巴斯特死的时候多大了？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You don't remember anything. He was 14 years old.
sentences.zh: 你什么都不记得了。他当时14岁。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How are the kids?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: They're doing well.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How old are they now?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Mike is 18.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: He's in college.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Janet is 16.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She's in high school.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Wow, they're growing up.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: They're growing up too fast.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How are the kids doing?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: They are so busy with sports.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What do they play?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Jenny plays softball and Luke plays baseball.
sentences.zh: 珍妮打垒球，卢克打棒球。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You must be busy with games.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: There are games every weekend.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Isn't it fun to watch?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It is so much fun to watch and cheer the teams on.
sentences.zh: 观看并为团队加油真是太有趣了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I miss those days.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: My kids are all in college now.
sentences.zh: 我的孩子们现在都上大学了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I am so busy.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What's going on?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have an important report due tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 我有一份重要的报告明天到期。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you almost done?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, and I'm starting to feel sick.
sentences.zh: 不，我开始觉得不舒服了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You better get some rest before you get sicker.
sentences.zh: 你最好在病情加重之前休息一下。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I don't have time to rest.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What are you doing this weekend?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I have so much to do.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why are you always so busy?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Because I have two jobs and I go to school part-time.
sentences.zh: 因为我有两份工作，我去上学做兼职。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm sorry. Did I make you upset?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I just have a lot of stress.
sentences.zh: 不，我只是有很大的压力。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can I help you?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Not really. It's just work and school stuff.
sentences.zh: 也不是，就是工作和学校的事。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You need to make time for yourself.
sentences.zh: 你需要为自己腾出时间。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know. Let's get dinner together next weekend.
sentences.zh: 我知道，我们下周末一起吃晚饭吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Have you met Joanne, my new roommate?
sentences.zh: 你见过我的新室友乔安妮吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I have not.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'll introduce you when she gets home.
sentences.zh: 她回家后我会给你介绍一下。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay, is she nice?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She's really nice.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: She's our age too.
sentences.zh: 她也是我们这个年纪的人。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Cool, we should take her to the coffee shop later.
sentences.zh: 好的，我们以后应该带她去咖啡店。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Great idea.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to introduce you to my new roommate.
sentences.zh: 我要给你介绍一下我的新室友。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What is her name?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Maria. She's from Spain.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What is she doing here?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She's a foreign exchange student.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's cool.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She's really nice. She's taught me some Spanish words.
sentences.zh: 她真的很好。她教了我一些西班牙语单词。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm taking Spanish right now.
sentences.zh: 我现在正在学习西班牙语。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You should try speaking Spanish to her.
sentences.zh: 你应该试着和她说西班牙语。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'll try. Hopefully she won't laugh at me.
sentences.zh: 我试试看，希望她不会嘲笑我。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you want to go to Lisa's party?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, not really.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's tell her we're sick.
sentences.zh: 让我们告诉她我们生病了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Both of us are sick?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yeah, we're roommates. She'll believe it.
sentences.zh: 是的，我们是室友。她会相信的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I feel bad about lying, but I really don't want to go to her party.
sentences.zh: 我撒谎很不好意思，但我真的不想去她的聚会。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you want to go to the concert tonight?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Do you have tickets?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, I got two tickets from my boss. She can't go.
sentences.zh: 是的，我从老板那里拿到了两张票。她不能去。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I would love to go, but what about Cindy?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I only have two tickets.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What if she finds out? She'll be so sad.
sentences.zh: 万一她发现了怎么办？她会很伤心的。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I can't give up these tickets. They are front row seats.
sentences.zh: 我不能放弃这些门票。它们是前排座位。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Front row?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's tell Cindy that we have to work late.
sentences.zh: 让我们告诉辛迪，我们要加班。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Don't you want to buy a concert t-shirt?
sentences.zh: 你不想买一件演唱会T恤吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Then she'll know we went to the concert.
sentences.zh: 然后她就会知道我们去了音乐会。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We'll get her one and tell her that my boss got them for us.
sentences.zh: 我们会给她买一个，告诉她我老板为我们买的。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Okay, that's a good lie.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can you help me move some furniture?
sentences.zh: 你能帮我搬一些家具吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I hurt my back.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When did you hurt your back?
sentences.zh: 你什么时候背部受伤的？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yesterday.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Really? I saw you playing tennis at the gym this morning.
sentences.zh: 真的吗，我看到你今天早上在健身房打网球。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry. I was trying to get out of helping.
sentences.zh: 对不起，我只是想摆脱帮助。

lessons.title: Lesson 74 A Ride to the Airport

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Hey, can you give me a ride to the airport tomorrow morning?
sentences.zh: 嘿，明天早上你能载我去机场吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry, but my car is in the shop.
sentences.zh: 抱歉，我的车还在修理厂。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How long has it been there?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: A couple of days. Why do you ask?
sentences.zh: 好几天了。你问这个干嘛？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's interesting. I saw you washing your car this morning.
sentences.zh: 有意思。我今天早上还看到你在洗车呢。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry. I lied. I just hate morning traffic.
sentences.zh: 对不起，我撒谎了。我只是讨厌早高峰的交通。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Come on. I'll take a taxi then.
sentences.zh: 算了吧。那我自己打车去。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's go to the party.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't feel like it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It's Friday night.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I want to watch movies in my pajamas.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Come on.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Brad is going to be there.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'll go for an hour.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You need a new look.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What's wrong with my look?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You should cut your hair. It's old-fashioned.
sentences.zh: 你应该剪头发。这是老式的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I like my long hair.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Try something new.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm afraid to.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's just hair. It'll grow back.
sentences.zh: 只是头发而已。它会长回来的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Maybe I will. It's hard to maintain this long hair.
sentences.zh: 也许我会。很难保持这么长的头发。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's go to the salon now.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay. You talked me into it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm thinking about getting a motorcycle.
sentences.zh: 我在考虑买一辆摩托车。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you crazy? Motorcycles are dangerous.
sentences.zh: 你疯了吗，摩托车很危险。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'll be careful.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I really wish you would not get one.
sentences.zh: 我真的希望你不会得到一个。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why? What's the problem?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I would be worried all the time.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Okay, I won't get one. I'll find something else to do.
sentences.zh: 好吧，我不会得到一个。我会找其他事情做。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's sell the house.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why? I love this house.
sentences.zh: 为什么？我喜欢这栋房子。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's too much work to take care of.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We raised four kids in this house.
sentences.zh: 我们在这栋房子里养大了四个孩子。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: They're all grown up now.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: There would be no memories in a new house.
sentences.zh: 新房子里不会有这些回忆。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We would make new memories. I really don't want to move, Carl.
sentences.zh: 我们会创造新的回忆。我真的不想搬家，卡尔。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay. I understand. You talked me out of it.
sentences.zh: 好吧。我理解。你说服我了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did you see that?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, it was an amazing magic trick.
sentences.zh: 是的，这是一个了不起的魔术。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It looked so real.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It did, but it was just magic.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you sure?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, don't believe everything you see.
sentences.zh: 是的，不要相信你所看到的一切。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did you see Jan's face?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, she looked very surprised.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She is faking it.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What do you mean?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She knew about the surprise party.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: How do you know?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Betty accidentally told her about it.
sentences.zh: 贝蒂不小心把这件事告诉了她。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you sure? She looked really surprised to me.
sentences.zh: 你确定吗？她看起来真的很惊讶。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She's a great actress. I guess you can't believe everything you see.
sentences.zh: 她是一位伟大的演员。我猜你不能相信你所看到的一切。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I can't understand why everyone wants to see this movie.
sentences.zh: 我不明白为什么每个人都想看这部电影。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It's really funny.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It's not funny at all.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Have you seen it?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes. It was boring.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You don't have a sense of humor.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I can't understand why everyone is so mad at John.
sentences.zh: 我不明白为什么每个人都对约翰这么生气。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: He was rude to Tom.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I don't think he was rude.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: He said mean things to Tom.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Tom asked John a question.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: John didn't have to be so honest.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: John was just telling the truth.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Sometimes the truth is painful.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Then you shouldn't ask questions.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I can't understand why you're on John's side.
sentences.zh: 我不明白你为什么站在约翰这边。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm sick and tired of fixing the dishwasher.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Maybe it's time to buy a new one.
sentences.zh: 也许是时候买一个新的了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can we afford to buy a new one?
sentences.zh: 我们能买得起一个新的吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It costs more money to fix every month.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You're right.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Let's go shopping for a new dishwasher today.
sentences.zh: 我们今天去买一台新的洗碗机吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm sick and tired of the noisy neighbor upstairs.
sentences.zh: 我厌倦了楼上吵闹的邻居。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: His music is so loud.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's talk to the apartment manager again.
sentences.zh: 让我们再次与公寓经理交谈。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: He's not going to do anything.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Then we should move.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: But the rent is cheap here.
sentences.zh: 但是这里的租金很便宜。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Well, we have to choose between cheap rent or a quiet apartment.
sentences.zh: 嗯，我们得在便宜的租金和安静的公寓之间做个选择。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's a tough choice.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, it's not. We can find another cheap place.
sentences.zh: 不，不难。我们可以再找个便宜的地方。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What if we find another place with a noisy neighbor?
sentences.zh: 要是我们找的另一个地方也有吵闹的邻居呢？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This hotel is so old. It's not that bad.
sentences.zh: 这家酒店太旧了。其实也没那么糟。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: This place is awful. There's a bug in my bed.
sentences.zh: 这地方太糟糕了。我床上有只虫子。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Okay, this place is pretty bad.
sentences.zh: 好吧，这地方确实挺糟的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: And the air conditioner is broken.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Did you tell the front desk?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Yes, but they said no rooms are available.
sentences.zh: 说了，但他们说没有空房间了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Let's ask for a refund when we check out.
sentences.zh: 那我们退房的时候要求退款吧。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Good idea. I'll write a review, too.
sentences.zh: 好主意。我还要写条差评。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I can't believe people like this show.
sentences.zh: 真不敢相信有人喜欢这个节目。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Stop complaining. It's not that bad.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Is there anything else to watch?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I like this show. Give it a chance.
sentences.zh: 我喜欢这个节目。给它个机会吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: There is just too much singing.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It's a musical. They're supposed to be singing.
sentences.zh: 这是音乐剧。本来就该唱歌的。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: How can you watch this?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I like musicals. Are you going to continue complaining?
sentences.zh: 我喜欢音乐剧。你还要继续抱怨吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No, I'll stop. But I get to choose the show next time.
sentences.zh: 不了，我不说了。但下次我来选节目。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay, that's a deal. Now stop complaining.
sentences.zh: 好，一言为定。现在别抱怨了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I'm failing my English class.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I don't understand what is going on.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Did you talk to your teacher about it?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She's the problem.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What's the problem?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She speaks too fast.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't understand what she's saying.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you ready for the English test tomorrow?
sentences.zh: 明天的英语考试准备好了吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I'm going to fail it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Didn't you study and take notes?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: My notes will not help me.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: The teacher speaks too fast.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I couldn't write anything down.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Why didn't you ask her to speak slowly?
sentences.zh: 你为什么不让她说慢点？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I was too embarrassed to tell her I didn't understand.
sentences.zh: 我太不好意思跟她说我听不懂了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'll help you study.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have a lot of notes.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why are you late again?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry, there was a lot of traffic.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's always your excuse.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I know, I left too late.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Then you should leave earlier.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'll try next time.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why are you always late?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You are always sorry.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What can I say?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What is your excuse this time?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I didn't hear my alarm clock.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: We're meeting for lunch, not breakfast.
sentences.zh: 我们是吃午饭，又不是吃早饭。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I went to sleep very late.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Were you studying for a test?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I was at a party last night.
sentences.zh: 不是，我昨晚去参加派对了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You have terrible excuses for being late.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have been waiting for this elevator for a long time.
sentences.zh: 我等这趟电梯等了好久了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What's wrong with it?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It is so slow.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Maybe it's not working.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I can hear it stopping on other floors.
sentences.zh: 我能听到它在其他楼层停。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Be patient. It's a very old building.
sentences.zh: 耐心点。这是一栋很旧的建筑。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Why is this elevator so slow? How long have you been waiting?
sentences.zh: 这电梯怎么这么慢？你等了多久了？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: About ten minutes.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This is a very old building. I don't have time to wait. I'm going to take the stairs.
sentences.zh: 这是栋很老的建筑。我没时间等了。我走楼梯。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We're on the 18th floor. But I'll come with you.
sentences.zh: 我们在18楼啊。不过我陪你一起走。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Okay, but you're wearing high heels. Good luck.
sentences.zh: 好吧，但你穿着高跟鞋呢。祝你好运。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'll take them off. Let's race.
sentences.zh: 我脱了就行。我们比赛吧。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are you ready to go?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't feel like going out tonight.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You always change your mind at the last minute.
sentences.zh: 你总是在最后一刻改变主意。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry. I'm just really tired today.
sentences.zh: 对不起。我今天真的很累。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: But I already bought the concert tickets.
sentences.zh: 但我已经买了演唱会门票了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Can you call one of your friends?
sentences.zh: 你能叫你的朋友一起去吗？

lessons.title: Lesson 94 Wait! I Changed My Mind

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think I want the blue car instead.
sentences.zh: 我想还是买那辆蓝色的车吧。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You like the blue one better. You always change your mind.
sentences.zh: 你更喜欢蓝色的。你总是变来变去。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: This is an expensive purchase.
sentences.zh: 这可是一笔不小的开销。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What if you change your mind again after we buy it? I promise I won't change my mind.
sentences.zh: 要是买了之后你又变卦怎么办？我保证我不会变主意。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Okay, let's buy the blue one.
sentences.zh: 好吧，那我们就买蓝色的。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You stole money from her purse.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No, I did not.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I saw you do it.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Are you calling me a liar?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, you are lying about stealing.
sentences.zh: 是的，你在偷东西这件事上撒谎了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You have no proof.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You stole my project idea.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I did not. That was my idea.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I came up with that idea during our meeting yesterday.
sentences.zh: 我是在昨天开会时想出那个创意的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't remember that.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You are a liar. You said it was a great idea.
sentences.zh: 你是个骗子。你说过那个创意很好。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: It's too late. I already presented the idea to the manager.
sentences.zh: 太迟了。我已经向经理展示了这个创意。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I am going to tell her that you stole my idea.
sentences.zh: 我要告诉她你偷了我的创意。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You don't have any proof.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes, I do. I recorded our meeting.
sentences.zh: 我有。我录了我们开会的音频。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You are very sneaky.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Khaled, your English is so good now.
sentences.zh: 哈立德，你的英语现在真好。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I've been going to ESL classes at night.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I am very impressed.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Thank you. I have a question to ask you.
sentences.zh: 谢谢。我有个问题想问你。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Please, ask me.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Would you like to go on a date?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Your English is so good now.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I bought an ESL teaching CD and I visit ESL websites.
sentences.zh: 我买了一张ESL教学光盘，还经常上ESL网站。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Are they helpful?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: They are very helpful.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You learn very fast.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I practice every day.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I want to become a US citizen.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You should have no problem passing the test.
sentences.zh: 你通过考试应该没问题。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I want to take the test next year.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I can help if you need help.
sentences.zh: 需要帮忙的话我可以帮你。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That would be wonderful.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You are a great friend.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: May I speak with the store manager, please?
sentences.zh: 请问我能和店长说话吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I am the store manager. How can I help you?
sentences.zh: 我就是店长。有什么可以帮你的？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: One of your sales clerks was very rude to me.
sentences.zh: 你们的一个售货员对我非常无礼。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm very sorry to hear that. Could you please tell me which clerk?
sentences.zh: 非常抱歉听到这个。能告诉我是哪个售货员吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: His name is Bob. He works in the mornings.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will talk to Bob as soon as possible.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can I help you?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I ordered a table a few weeks ago, but it still has not arrived.
sentences.zh: 我几周前订了一张桌子，但到现在还没到。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you have the order number or your receipt?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: No. Unfortunately, I lost them both.
sentences.zh: 没有。不幸的是，我都弄丢了。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I see. I can try to look it up with your address or phone number.
sentences.zh: 我明白了。我可以用你的地址或电话号码查一下。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Here is both my address and phone number.
sentences.zh: 这是我的地址和电话号码。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I found your item. It was delivered to a different address.
sentences.zh: 我找到你的订单了。它被送到了另一个地址。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: So what will you do about it?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I am very sorry for the error.
sentences.zh: 非常抱歉出了这个差错。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: We will send a replacement table to your correct address as soon as possible.
sentences.zh: 我们会尽快把替换的桌子送到你的正确地址。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Thank you for your wonderful customer service.
sentences.zh: 谢谢你们这么好的服务。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Do you think you can get this stain out?
sentences.zh: 你觉得这个污渍能洗掉吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I think I should take it to the dry cleaners.
sentences.zh: 我觉得应该送去干洗店。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That is my favorite shirt.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You should be more careful with it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I know. Do you think the dry cleaners can get it out?
sentences.zh: 我知道。你觉得干洗店能洗掉吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I don't know. Let's ask them.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have an emergency. I spilled coffee on my dress and I need it dry cleaned as soon as possible.
sentences.zh: 我有急事。我把咖啡洒在裙子上了，需要尽快干洗。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: When do you need it?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Tonight.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: That's very short notice. We have a lot of customers this week.
sentences.zh: 时间太紧了。我们这周客人很多。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It is my wedding anniversary and I would really like to wear this dress.
sentences.zh: 今天是我的结婚纪念日，我很想穿这条裙子。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Wow, that is a big stain.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I am desperate. I will pay extra.
sentences.zh: 我真的很急。我愿意多付钱。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You have been a good customer for many years. I will do it for no extra charge.
sentences.zh: 你是我们多年的老客户了。不额外收费。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Thank you so much. I will tell all of my friends about this place.
sentences.zh: 太感谢了。我会告诉所有朋友你们这家店。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Thank you too. It will be ready by 4 p.m.
sentences.zh: 也谢谢你。下午4点可以取。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I would like to make an appointment to see Dr. Smith.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What do you need to see the doctor for?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I just need an annual checkup.
sentences.zh: 我只是需要做年度体检。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay. The doctor's next available appointment is next Monday at 10 a.m.
sentences.zh: 好的。医生下一个可预约的时间是下周一上午10点。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's perfect. Do I need to bring anything with me?
sentences.zh: 太好了。我需要带什么吗？

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Please bring your insurance card and photo ID.
sentences.zh: 请带好保险卡和带照片的身份证件。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I need to see Dr. Johnson right away.
sentences.zh: 我需要马上看约翰逊医生。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What is wrong?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think I broke my arm.
sentences.zh: 我觉得我的手臂骨折了。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Can you move it?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: No. It hurts if I try to move it.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: You need to go to the emergency room.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: The emergency room is too expensive. I do not have medical insurance.
sentences.zh: 急诊太贵了。我没有医疗保险。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Do you have someone who can bring you here?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Yes. My friend is going to drive.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay. Dr. Johnson will take a look at your arm.
sentences.zh: 好的。约翰逊医生会看一下你的手臂。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Please come as soon as possible.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I would like to make a haircut appointment.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Our next available appointment is today at 3 p.m.
sentences.zh: 我们最近可以约今天下午3点。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I do not get off work until 5 p.m.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: How about tomorrow at 5.30 p.m.?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's perfect.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Great. I'll send you a reminder tomorrow morning.
sentences.zh: 好的。我明早给你发个提醒。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I would like to make a haircut appointment with Anne.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Anne does not work here anymore.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: When did Anne leave?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: She was a great hairstylist.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She moved out of the state last month.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Is there anyone else you recommend?
sentences.zh: 你还有推荐的其他人吗？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Jane is a great stylist.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Okay, I will try her.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: She has an opening tomorrow at 4 p.m.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will take it.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have a problem with my online order.
sentences.zh: 我的线上订单有点问题。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What is the problem?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I ordered a black jacket, but I received a white one.
sentences.zh: 我订的是黑色夹克，但收到的是白色的。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm very sorry to hear that.
sentences.zh: 非常抱歉听到这个消息。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: Can you exchange the jacket for me?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: How do I return the wrong one?
sentences.zh: 我怎么把这件错的退回去？

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: You can return it by mail or return it to one of our stores.
sentences.zh: 你可以邮寄退回，也可以到我们的任意门店退回。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I have a problem with my online order.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: What is the problem?

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I ordered red roses for my wife, but she received daisies.
sentences.zh: 我给妻子订了红玫瑰，但她收到的是雏菊。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I'm sorry to hear that.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: It is our anniversary, and roses are her favorite flowers.
sentences.zh: 今天是我们的纪念日，玫瑰是她最喜欢的花。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I will have red roses delivered to her as soon as possible.
sentences.zh: 我会尽快把红玫瑰送给她。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What should I do with the daisies?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: Please keep them, since we made the error.
sentences.zh: 请留着吧，是我们的失误。

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: That's very nice of you.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I hope your wife enjoys both flower bouquets.
sentences.zh: 希望你妻子喜欢两束花。

lessons.title: Lesson 109 Getting a Bite to Eat

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I am hungry.

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I am hungry too.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: What are you going to order?

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: I am going to have a hamburger and french fries.

sentences.speaker: A
sentences.en: I think I am going to order a turkey sandwich.
sentences.zh: 我想点一个火鸡三明治。

sentences.speaker: B
sentences.en: The food here is delicious.

sentences.speaker: A
